name: Bourgogne-Franche-Comte (France)

colors.symbolism: the gold (or) field of the historic arms of both Burgundy and Franche-Comte
the azure of the lion of Franche-Comte and of the bordure compony of Burgundy
details of the lion's tongue and claws and elements of the Burgundian bordure
highlights and details of the heraldic charges

description: A composite armorial banner combining the historic arms of Burgundy and Franche-Comte on a predominantly gold field, with red, blue, white, and black detailing.
history: Bourgogne-Franche-Comte is a French region created by the territorial reform of 2016, merging the former regions of Burgundy (Bourgogne) and Franche-Comte. France traditionally does not promote regional flags, and this composite heraldic banner is used in a de-facto capacity rather than as an officially adopted regional flag.

features.symbolism: A composite armorial banner combining the historic arms of Burgundy (Bourgogne) and Franche-Comte, the two former regions merged in 2016.

features.role: the combined arms of Bourgogne (semé-de-lis with bordure compony) and Franche-Comte (gold field with crowned lion azure)
features.symbolism: The merged region's arms juxtapose the historic Burgundian bordure-compony fleurs-de-lis pattern with the rampant lion of Franche-Comte, recording the 2016 merger of the two former regions.
